pages.privacyPolicy:
**Operator:** Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack  
**Jurisdiction:** Singapore  
**Contact:** support@policypack.org  
**Effective Date:** April 10, 2026

---

## 1. Introduction and Scope

This Privacy Policy describes how Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd., trading as PolicyPack ("PolicyPack," "we," "us," or "our"), collects, uses, discloses, and protects personal information when you access or use the PolicyPack platform at https://policypack.org (the "Service"). This policy applies to all users, including visitors, registered account holders, and subscribers, regardless of geographic location.

PolicyPack is an AI-powered legal automation platform designed for SaaS companies. We help founders and operators generate, publish, and maintain compliance disclosures such as Privacy Policies, Terms of Service, Cookie Policies, and Refund Policies. Our Service is available globally, including to users in the United States, United Kingdom, European Union, and Singapore.

We are the data controller for personal information processed through the Service. If you have questions about this policy or wish to exercise your privacy rights, contact us at support@policypack.org.

---

## 2. Information We Collect

We collect personal information directly from you, automatically through your use of the Service, and in limited cases from third parties.

### 2.1 Information You Provide

**Account Registration:** When you create an account, we collect your full name, email address, company name, and password. If you register through a third-party authentication provider such as Google or GitHub, we receive your name, email address, and profile identifier from that provider.

**Company and Product Information:** To generate compliance documents, you provide details about your business, including legal entity name, jurisdiction, website domain, product description, data processing activities, payment methods, and target markets. This information is stored in your account and used to populate your generated policies.

**Payment Information:** When you subscribe to a paid plan, our payment processor collects your billing name, email address, payment card details, and billing address. We do not store complete payment card numbers on our servers. We receive transaction identifiers, subscription status, and billing history from the payment processor.

**Support and Communications:** When you contact us for support, we collect your name, email address, account details, and the content of your messages. We may retain correspondence history to improve our Service and resolve your inquiries.

**Feedback and Survey Responses:** If you participate in surveys or provide feedback, we collect your responses and any personal information you choose to include.

### 2.2 Information Collected Automatically

**Usage Data:** We collect information about your interactions with the Service, including pages viewed, features accessed, documents generated, time spent on the platform, and navigation paths. We log IP addresses, browser type and version, operating system, device identifiers, and referring URLs.

**Cookies and Similar Technologies:** We use cookies, web beacons, and similar tracking technologies to recognize your browser, maintain your session, remember your preferences, and analyze usage patterns. For detailed information about our use of cookies, including categories, purposes, and your control options, see our Cookie Policy at https://policypack.org/cookie-policy.

**Analytics Data:** We use analytics services to understand how users interact with the Service. These services collect information such as page views, session duration, bounce rates, and conversion events. Analytics providers may set their own cookies and collect data according to their privacy policies.

### 2.3 Information from Third Parties

**Authentication Providers:** If you sign in using Google, GitHub, or another third-party service, we receive your name, email address, and profile information from that provider.

**Payment Processors:** Our payment processor shares transaction status, subscription details, and billing events with us to manage your account and provide the Service.

**Public Sources:** We may supplement company information you provide with publicly available data, such as business registration details or website metadata, to improve the accuracy of generated documents.

---

## 3. How We Use Your Information

We use personal information to provide, improve, and protect the Service, and to communicate with you.

### 3.1 Service Delivery

We use your account information, company details, and product data to generate customized compliance documents. Secure automated data processors analyze your inputs and produce policies tailored to your business model, jurisdiction, and regulatory requirements. We store your generated documents in your account for retrieval, editing, and export.

We use your email address to send transactional messages, including account verification, password resets, subscription confirmations, billing notifications, and document generation alerts.

### 3.2 Payment Processing

We share your billing information with our payment processor to process subscription payments, manage billing cycles, handle refunds, and prevent fraud. The payment processor acts as a merchant of record and processes payments on our behalf.

### 3.3 Platform Improvement

We analyze usage data to understand how users interact with the Service, identify popular features, diagnose technical issues, and prioritize product development. We use aggregated and anonymized data to improve document generation accuracy, refine user interface design, and optimize platform performance.

### 3.4 Security and Fraud Prevention

We monitor account activity, login attempts, and usage patterns to detect and prevent unauthorized access, fraudulent transactions, and abuse of the Service. We may use IP addresses, device identifiers, and behavioral signals to identify suspicious activity.

### 3.5 Customer Support

We use your contact information and support correspondence to respond to inquiries, troubleshoot technical problems, and provide assistance with account management and document generation.

### 3.6 Legal Compliance

We process personal information to comply with legal obligations, respond to lawful requests from government authorities, enforce our Terms of Service, protect our rights and property, and defend against legal claims.

### 3.7 Marketing Communications

With your consent, we may send promotional emails about new features, product updates, and special offers. You may opt out of marketing communications at any time by clicking the unsubscribe link in any promotional email or by contacting support@policypack.org. Transactional messages related to your account and subscriptions are not subject to opt-out.

---

## 4. Legal Bases for Processing

We process personal information under the following legal bases, depending on your location and the nature of the processing activity.

### 4.1 Contractual Necessity

We process your account information, company details, and payment data to perform our contract with you and provide the Service you requested. Without this information, we cannot create your account, generate documents, or process payments.

### 4.2 Legitimate Interests

We process usage data, analytics information, and security logs based on our legitimate interests in operating, improving, and securing the Service. We have assessed that these interests are not overridden by your privacy rights. Our legitimate interests include:

- Improving platform functionality and user experience
- Detecting and preventing fraud and security threats
- Analyzing usage trends to prioritize product development
- Providing customer support and resolving technical issues
- Enforcing our Terms of Service and protecting our legal rights

### 4.3 Consent

We process certain categories of data based on your explicit consent, including:

- Marketing communications (you may withdraw consent at any time)
- Optional cookies and analytics tracking (you may manage preferences in our Cookie Policy)
- Third-party authentication (you control whether to link external accounts)

### 4.4 Legal Obligations

We process personal information to comply with applicable laws, regulations, and legal processes, including tax reporting, fraud prevention, and responses to lawful government requests.

---

## 5. How We Share Your Information

We do not sell personal information. We share personal information only in the limited circumstances described below.

### 5.1 Service Providers and Subprocessors

We engage third-party service providers to perform functions on our behalf. These providers act as data processors and are contractually obligated to process personal information only as instructed by us and to implement appropriate security measures.

**Secure Automated Data Processors:** We use advanced automated data processing systems to analyze your inputs and generate compliance documents. These systems process company information, product descriptions, and regulatory requirements to produce customized policies. Data submitted for document generation is processed securely and is not used to train or improve third-party models without your explicit consent.

**Payment Processors:** We share billing information with our payment processor, which acts as merchant of record and processes subscription payments, manages billing cycles, and handles refunds. The payment processor collects and stores payment card details according to PCI DSS standards.

**Hosting and Infrastructure Providers:** We use cloud hosting services to store account data, generated documents, and usage logs. These providers maintain data centers with physical and technical security controls.

**Analytics Providers:** We share usage data with analytics services to measure platform performance, understand user behavior, and identify areas for improvement. Analytics providers may set cookies and collect data according to their own privacy policies.

**Email Service Providers:** We use email delivery services to send transactional and marketing messages. These providers process your email address and message content to deliver communications on our behalf.

**Customer Support Tools:** We use support ticketing and communication platforms to manage customer inquiries. These tools process your contact information and correspondence history.

We maintain a public list of subprocessors at https://policypack.org/subprocessors. We will update this list before engaging new subprocessors and provide notice of material changes.

### 5.2 Business Transfers

If PolicyPack is involved in a merger, acquisition, asset sale, or bankruptcy proceeding, personal information may be transferred to the acquiring entity or successor organization. We will provide notice before your information is transferred and becomes subject to a different privacy policy.

### 5.3 Legal Requirements

We may disclose personal information if required by law, regulation, legal process, or government request. We may also disclose information to enforce our Terms of Service, protect our rights and property, investigate fraud or security issues, or protect the safety of our users or the public.

### 5.4 With Your Consent

We may share personal information with third parties when you provide explicit consent, such as when you authorize integration with external services or request that we share data with your legal or compliance advisors.

---

## 6. International Data Transfers

PolicyPack operates globally and may transfer personal information to countries outside your jurisdiction, including countries that may not provide the same level of data protection as your home country.

### 6.1 Transfers from the European Economic Area and United Kingdom

If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA) or United Kingdom, we transfer personal information to service providers and subprocessors located outside the EEA and UK. We implement appropriate safeguards to protect your information, including:

- Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission and UK Information Commissioner's Office
- Data Processing Agreements with subprocessors that include data protection obligations
- Technical and organizational measures to ensure data security during transfer and storage

### 6.2 Transfers from Other Jurisdictions

If you are located outside the EEA or UK, we transfer personal information in accordance with applicable data protection laws in your jurisdiction. We implement contractual and technical safeguards to protect your information regardless of where it is processed.

---

## 7. Data Retention

We retain personal information for as long as necessary to provide the Service, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements.

### 7.1 Account Data

We retain your account information, company details, and generated documents for the duration of your active subscription. If you cancel your subscription or delete your account, we retain your data for 30 days to allow for account reactivation or data recovery. After 30 days, we permanently delete your account data, except as required for legal or regulatory compliance.

### 7.2 Backup Retention

Deleted data may persist in backup systems for up to 90 days. Backups are stored securely and are not accessible for operational purposes. After 90 days, deleted data is purged from backup systems.

### 7.3 Usage and Analytics Data

We retain usage logs and analytics data for up to 24 months to analyze platform performance and improve the Service. After 24 months, we aggregate or anonymize this data so that it can no longer identify individual users.

### 7.4 Payment Records

We retain payment transaction records, billing history, and invoices for up to seven years to comply with tax and accounting regulations.

### 7.5 Support Correspondence

We retain customer support correspondence for up to three years to improve our support processes and resolve recurring issues.

### 7.6 Legal Holds

If personal information is subject to a legal hold, regulatory investigation, or pending litigation, we retain that information until the hold is lifted or the matter is resolved, even if it would otherwise be deleted under our standard retention schedule.

---

## 8. Your Privacy Rights

Depending on your location, you may have rights regarding your personal information. We honor these rights regardless of where you are located.

### 8.1 Access and Portability

You have the right to request a copy of the personal information we hold about you. You may export your account data, company information, and generated documents at any time through your account settings. If you need assistance or require data in a specific format, contact support@policypack.org.

### 8.2 Correction

You have the right to correct inaccurate or incomplete personal information. You may update your account details, company information, and preferences through your account settings. If you cannot make corrections yourself, contact support@policypack.org.

### 8.3 Deletion

You have the right to request deletion of your personal information. You may delete your account at any time through your account settings. Upon deletion, we will permanently remove your account data within 30 days, except as required for legal or regulatory compliance. Note that deleted data may persist in backup systems for up to 90 days.

### 8.4 Restriction and Objection

You have the right to restrict or object to certain processing activities. You may object to marketing communications by clicking the unsubscribe link in any promotional email. You may restrict analytics tracking by managing your cookie preferences in our Cookie Policy. If you wish to object to other processing activities, contact support@policypack.org.

### 8.5 Withdraw Consent

Where we process personal information based on your consent, you have the right to withdraw that consent at any time. Withdrawing consent does not affect the lawfulness of processing before withdrawal. You may withdraw consent for marketing communications, optional cookies, and third-party authentication through your account settings or by contacting support@policypack.org.

### 8.6 Lodge a Complaint

If you believe we have not handled your personal information in accordance with applicable law, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. If you are located in the EEA or UK, you may contact your local data protection authority. If you are located in Singapore, you may contact the Personal Data Protection Commission. If you are located in California, you may contact the California Privacy Protection Agency.

### 8.7 Non-Discrimination

We will not discriminate against you for exercising your privacy rights. You will not be denied service, charged different prices, or provided a lower quality of service because you exercised your rights.

### 8.8 Exercising Your Rights

To exercise any of these rights, contact us at support@policypack.org. Please include your account email address and a description of your request. We will respond within 30 days for most requests. For complex requests or high volumes of requests, we may extend our response time by an additional 30 days and will notify you of the extension.

We may request additional information to verify your identity before processing your request. This is a security measure to ensure that personal information is not disclosed to unauthorized parties.

---

## 9. California Privacy Rights

If you are a California resident, you have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA).

### 9.1 Categories of Personal Information Collected

In the preceding 12 months, we have collected the following categories of personal information:

- Identifiers (name, email address, IP address, device identifiers)
- Commercial information (subscription details, billing history, transaction records)
- Internet or network activity (usage data, browsing history, interaction with the Service)
- Professional or employment-related information (company name, job title, business details)
- Inferences drawn from the above categories (preferences, usage patterns, document generation history)

### 9.2 Business Purposes

We use personal information for the business purposes described in Section 3, including service delivery, payment processing, platform improvement, security and fraud prevention, customer support, legal compliance, and marketing communications.

### 9.3 Categories of Third Parties

We share personal information with the following categories of third parties:

- Service providers and subprocessors (secure automated data processors, payment processors, hosting providers, analytics providers, email service providers, customer support tools)
- Professional advisors (legal counsel, accountants, auditors)
- Government authorities (in response to lawful requests or legal obligations)
- Business transaction parties (in connection with mergers, acquisitions, or asset sales)

### 9.4 Do Not Sell or Share

We do not sell personal information. We do not share personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising. If our practices change, we will update this policy and provide a "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" link on our website.

### 9.5 Sensitive Personal Information

We do not collect or process sensitive personal information as defined by the CPRA, including Social Security numbers, driver's license numbers, financial account credentials, precise geolocation, racial or ethnic origin, religious beliefs, genetic data, biometric data for identification, health information, or sex life or sexual orientation.

### 9.6 Retention

We retain personal information as described in Section 7. We do not retain personal information for longer than reasonably necessary for the purposes for which it was collected.

### 9.7 Exercising California Rights

California residents may exercise the rights described in Section 8, including the right to know, delete, correct, and opt out. To exercise these rights, contact support@policypack.org or submit a request through your account settings. We will not discriminate against you for exercising your California privacy rights.

---

## 10. Security

We implement technical, administrative, and physical security measures to protect personal information from unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, and destruction.

### 10.1 Technical Safeguards

We use encryption to protect data in transit and at rest. All connections to the Service use TLS encryption. Passwords are hashed using industry-standard algorithms. We implement access controls, authentication mechanisms, and session management to prevent unauthorized access to accounts and data.

### 10.
pages.termsAndGdpr:
**Operator:** Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack  
**Jurisdiction:** Singapore  
**Website:** https://policypack.org  
**Support Contact:** support@policypack.org  
**Effective Date:** April 10, 2026

---

## 1. Acceptance and Operator Identity

These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of PolicyPack, an AI-powered legal automation platform operated by Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd., a company incorporated in Singapore and trading as PolicyPack. By creating an account, accessing the platform at policypack.org, or using any PolicyPack service, you agree to be bound by these Terms in their entirety. If you do not agree, you may not use the service.

PolicyPack provides tools to generate, publish, and maintain compliance disclosures including Privacy Policies, Terms of Service, Cookie Policies, Refund Policies, and related legal documents. The platform is designed for SaaS founders, website operators, payment providers, app reviewers, and privacy-conscious customers operating globally.

All references to "we," "us," or "our" refer to Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack. All references to "you" or "your" refer to the individual or entity accessing or using the service.

---

## 2. Eligibility and Account Use

You must be at least 18 years of age and possess the legal capacity to enter into binding contracts in your jurisdiction to use PolicyPack. If you are accessing the service on behalf of a company or other legal entity, you represent that you have the authority to bind that entity to these Terms.

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activity that occurs under your account. You agree to notify us immediately at support@policypack.org if you suspect unauthorized access or any security breach. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms or engage in fraudulent, abusive, or unlawful conduct.

Each account is licensed for use by a single organization or individual. You may not share, resell, or transfer your account without our prior written consent. Multi-user access within a single organization is permitted under applicable subscription plans.

---

## 3. Complimentary Launch Access, Subscriptions, Billing, and Refunds

PolicyPack may offer complimentary launch access to early users, allowing limited or full use of the platform without charge for a specified period. Complimentary access is provided at our sole discretion and may be modified, suspended, or terminated at any time without notice. No refunds or credits are owed for complimentary access.

Paid subscriptions are billed on a recurring basis according to the plan you select. By subscribing, you authorize us and our payment processors to charge your designated payment method at the start of each billing cycle. Subscription fees are non-refundable except as required by applicable consumer protection law or as explicitly stated in our Refund Policy.

If you upgrade or downgrade your subscription, changes take effect at the start of the next billing cycle unless otherwise specified. If you cancel your subscription, you retain access to paid features until the end of the current billing period. No partial refunds are provided for mid-cycle cancellations unless required by law.

Payment processing is handled by third-party merchant-of-record services and payment processors. You agree to comply with their terms and policies. We are not responsible for payment processing errors, declined transactions, or disputes arising from your payment method.

For residents of the European Union and United Kingdom, you have a statutory right to withdraw from digital service contracts within 14 days of purchase. By using the service immediately upon subscription, you expressly waive this right and acknowledge that no refund is owed if you cancel after accessing paid features. For all other refund inquiries, contact support@policypack.org.

---

## 4. Service Scope and Acceptable Use

PolicyPack provides automated tools to assist in drafting compliance disclosures and legal documents. The platform uses secure automated data processors to analyze your inputs, apply legal frameworks, and generate publication-ready content. You retain full editorial control over all outputs and are responsible for reviewing, customizing, and publishing documents generated through the service.

You agree to use PolicyPack only for lawful purposes and in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. You may not use the service to:

- Generate documents for unlawful, fraudulent, or deceptive purposes
- Violate the intellectual property rights of any third party
- Harass, defame, or harm any individual or entity
- Distribute malware, viruses, or other harmful code
- Attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, or extract source code from the platform
- Overload, disrupt, or interfere with the operation of the service or its infrastructure
- Access or use the service through automated means without our prior written consent, except through documented API endpoints where available

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate access for any user who violates these acceptable use provisions or engages in conduct that we determine, in our sole discretion, to be harmful to the platform, other users, or our business interests.

---

## 5. AI-Assisted Outputs and No Legal Advice Boundary

PolicyPack uses secure automated data processors to generate legal and compliance content based on your inputs and selected jurisdictions. These outputs are provided as informational tools to assist in drafting and are not a substitute for professional legal advice.

We do not guarantee that any document generated through PolicyPack will be legally sufficient, enforceable, or compliant with the laws of any jurisdiction. Legal requirements vary by industry, jurisdiction, and individual circumstances. You are solely responsible for reviewing all generated content, consulting qualified legal counsel where appropriate, and ensuring that published documents meet your specific legal obligations.

No attorney-client relationship is created between you and Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd., PolicyPack, or any of our employees, contractors, or automated systems. We do not provide legal advice, legal representation, or legal opinions. Any reliance on generated content is at your own risk.

We make no representations or warranties regarding the accuracy, completeness, or suitability of any output for your particular use case. You acknowledge that automated systems may produce errors, omissions, or content that requires human review and customization.

---

## 6. Customer Responsibilities

You are responsible for the accuracy and completeness of all information you provide to PolicyPack, including company details, jurisdictional selections, data processing activities, and policy content. Inaccurate or incomplete inputs may result in outputs that do not meet your legal obligations.

You agree to:

- Review and customize all generated documents before publication
- Update published policies promptly when your business practices, data processing activities, or legal obligations change
- Maintain backup copies of all published documents
- Comply with all applicable laws governing your use of the service and publication of legal disclosures
- Notify us promptly of any errors, defects, or security vulnerabilities you discover in the platform

You are solely responsible for obtaining any necessary consents, authorizations, or approvals required to collect, process, or disclose personal data as described in your published policies. PolicyPack does not monitor or enforce your compliance with published policies.

---

## 7. Intellectual Property

PolicyPack and all associated software, content, trademarks, and materials are owned by Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. or our licensors and are protected by copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws. These Terms grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the service for your internal business purposes in accordance with these Terms.

You retain all intellectual property rights in the content you input into the platform and the customized documents you generate. By using the service, you grant us a limited license to process, store, and display your inputs solely for the purpose of providing the service to you. We do not claim ownership of your generated documents or use your inputs to train automated systems or provide services to other users without your explicit consent.

You may not copy, modify, distribute, sell, or lease any part of the platform or its underlying technology. You may not use our trademarks, logos, or branding without our prior written consent. Any unauthorized use of our intellectual property may result in termination of your account and legal action.

---

## 8. Third-Party Services

PolicyPack integrates with third-party services for payment processing, infrastructure hosting, and other operational functions. Your use of these third-party services is subject to their respective terms and privacy policies. We are not responsible for the availability, accuracy, or practices of third-party services.

We may provide links to third-party websites or resources for informational purposes. These links do not constitute an endorsement, and we are not responsible for the content, accuracy, or practices of any third-party site. You access third-party sites at your own risk.

If you choose to integrate PolicyPack with third-party platforms or services, you are responsible for ensuring that such integrations comply with the terms of service and privacy policies of those platforms.

---

## 9. Availability and Changes

We strive to provide reliable and continuous access to PolicyPack, but we do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free service. The platform may be temporarily unavailable due to maintenance, updates, technical issues, or circumstances beyond our control. We are not liable for any loss, damage, or inconvenience resulting from service interruptions.

We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any aspect of the service at any time, with or without notice. We may add, remove, or change features, pricing, or subscription plans. Material changes to these Terms will be communicated by email or through the platform at least 30 days before taking effect. Your continued use of the service after changes become effective constitutes acceptance of the modified Terms.

If you do not agree to any changes, you may terminate your account before the changes take effect. No refunds will be provided for prepaid subscription fees unless required by applicable consumer protection law.

---

## 10. Disclaimers and Liability Limits

PolicyPack is provided on an "as-is" and "as-available" basis without warranties of any kind, whether express, implied, or statutory. To the fullest extent permitted by law, we disclaim all warranties, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, and accuracy.

We do not warrant that the service will meet your requirements, operate without interruption, be error-free, or be free from viruses or other harmful components. We do not warrant that any generated content will be legally sufficient, enforceable, or compliant with applicable laws.

To the fullest extent permitted by law, Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd., its officers, directors, employees, and agents shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, data, goodwill, or business opportunities, arising out of or related to your use of or inability to use the service, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.

Our total liability to you for all claims arising out of or related to these Terms or your use of the service shall not exceed the amount you paid to us in the 12 months preceding the claim, or one hundred Singapore dollars, whichever is greater.

Nothing in these Terms excludes or limits liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, fraud, or any other liability that cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law. If you are a consumer in the European Union or United Kingdom, you retain all statutory rights that cannot be waived or limited by contract, including rights under consumer protection legislation.

---

## 11. Termination

You may terminate your account at any time by contacting support@policypack.org or using account closure tools provided in the platform. Upon termination, your access to paid features will continue until the end of the current billing period. No refunds will be provided for unused time unless required by applicable consumer protection law.

We may suspend or terminate your account immediately, without notice, if we determine in our sole discretion that you have violated these Terms, engaged in fraudulent or unlawful conduct, or pose a risk to the platform, other users, or our business interests. We may also terminate accounts that remain inactive for extended periods.

Upon termination, you will lose access to the platform and any content stored in your account. We recommend exporting all generated documents and data before terminating your account. We are not obligated to retain or provide access to your data after termination, except as required by applicable data protection law.

Provisions of these Terms that by their nature should survive termination, including intellectual property rights, disclaimers, liability limits, and dispute resolution provisions, shall remain in effect after termination.

---

## 12. Governing Law and Disputes

These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Singapore, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any dispute arising out of or related to these Terms or your use of the service shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Singapore.

If you are a consumer in the European Union or United Kingdom, you retain the right to bring proceedings in the courts of your country of residence, and nothing in these Terms affects your statutory rights under applicable consumer protection legislation.

Before initiating formal legal proceedings, you agree to contact us at support@policypack.org to attempt to resolve the dispute informally. We will make reasonable efforts to resolve disputes amicably within 30 days of receiving notice.

If you are a resident of the United States, you agree that any dispute not resolved informally shall be resolved through binding individual arbitration in accordance with the rules of a mutually agreed arbitration body, and you waive any right to participate in a class action lawsuit or class-wide arbitration. This arbitration provision does not apply to residents of jurisdictions where such waivers are unenforceable.

---

## 13. General Provisions

These Terms constitute the entire agreement between you and Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. regarding your use of PolicyPack and supersede all prior agreements, understandings, and communications, whether written or oral.

If any provision of these Terms is found to be invalid, unlawful, or unenforceable, that provision shall be severed, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

Our failure to enforce any right or provision of these Terms shall not constitute a waiver of that right or provision. No waiver of any term shall be deemed a further or continuing waiver of that term or any other term.

You may not assign or transfer these Terms or your account without our prior written consent. We may assign or transfer these Terms or any rights or obligations hereunder without restriction.

All notices to you will be sent to the email address associated with your account or posted on the platform. Notices to us must be sent to support@policypack.org. Notices are deemed received when sent by email or when posted on the platform.

These Terms are written in English. Any translations are provided for convenience only, and the English version shall prevail in the event of any conflict.

---

## Global Privacy Appendix: Cross-Border Processing, Data Roles, Security, and Rights

This appendix supplements the PolicyPack Privacy Policy and provides additional information regarding data processing roles, international transfers, security measures, and data subject rights applicable to users in multiple jurisdictions.

### A. Data Controller and Processor Roles

Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack acts as a data controller with respect to account information, billing data, and usage analytics collected directly from users. We determine the purposes and means of processing this data to operate the platform, provide customer support, and improve our services.

When you use PolicyPack to generate compliance documents, you act as the data controller for any personal data you input into the platform or describe in your generated policies. PolicyPack acts as a data processor, processing your inputs solely on your instructions to provide the document generation service.

For enterprise customers requiring formal data processing agreements, we offer standard contractual terms that define our obligations as a processor. Contact support@policypack.org to request a data processing addendum.

### B. International Data Transfers

PolicyPack operates globally and may transfer, store, and process your data in Singapore, the United States, the European Union, and other jurisdictions where we or our service providers maintain infrastructure. These transfers are necessary to provide the service and are conducted in accordance with applicable data protection laws.

For transfers of personal data from the European Economic Area, United Kingdom, or Switzerland to countries that do not provide an adequate level of data protection as determined by the European Commission or UK authorities, we rely on standard contractual clauses approved by the European Commission or UK Information Commissioner's Office. Copies of these clauses are available upon request at support@policypack.org.

We implement technical and organizational measures to ensure that transferred data receives a level of protection equivalent to that required under European and UK data protection law, including encryption in transit and at rest, access controls, and contractual obligations imposed on sub-processors.

### C. Security Measures

We implement industry-standard technical and organizational measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. These measures include:

- Encryption of data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher
- Encryption of data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent standards
- Role-based access controls limiting employee and contractor access to data
- Regular security audits and vulnerability assessments
- Secure authentication mechanisms including password hashing and optional multi-factor authentication
- Logging and monitoring of access to production systems
- Incident response procedures for detecting and responding to security breaches

No method of transmission or storage is completely secure. While we strive to protect your data, we cannot guarantee absolute security. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for any activity that occurs under your account.

In the event of a data breach that poses a risk to your rights and freedoms, we will notify affected users and relevant supervisory authorities in accordance with applicable data protection law, typically within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach.

### D. Data Retention

We retain account information, billing data, and usage logs for as long as your account remains active and for a reasonable period thereafter to comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements.

Generated documents and inputs are retained in your account until you delete them or terminate your account. Upon account termination, we will delete or anonymize your data within 90 days, except where retention is required by law or necessary to resolve disputes.

Backup copies of deleted data may persist in our backup systems for up to 90 days after deletion. Backup data is not accessible through the platform and is retained solely for disaster recovery purposes.

Aggregated and anonymized usage data that cannot be linked to an individual or organization may be retained indefinitely for analytics and service improvement purposes.

### E. Data Subject Rights

Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights with
pages.cookiePolicy:
**Operator:** Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack  
**Website:** https://policypack.org  
**Contact:** support@policypack.org  
**Jurisdiction:** Singapore  
**Last Updated:** April 10, 2026

This Cookie Policy explains how PolicyPack uses cookies and similar tracking technologies on the policypack.org website and within the PolicyPack customer workspace. It applies to all visitors, registered users, and subscribers accessing our platform from any jurisdiction.

## 1. What This Policy Covers

This policy describes the cookies and tracking technologies deployed by PolicyPack, their purposes, the data they collect, and the controls available to you. It supplements our Privacy Policy and should be read together with that document.

PolicyPack uses cookies to deliver core platform functionality, analyze usage patterns, and improve service performance. We do not use cookies for cross-site behavioral advertising or sell cookie data to third parties.

## 2. What Are Cookies

Cookies are small text files placed on your device by a web server. They enable websites to recognize your browser, remember preferences, and maintain session state across page loads.

Similar technologies include local storage, session storage, and software development kit identifiers used in mobile applications. This policy covers all such mechanisms collectively referred to as "cookies."

## 3. Cookie Categories

We classify cookies into the following categories based on their function and legal basis:

### 3.1 Strictly Necessary Cookies

These cookies are essential for the platform to function. They enable account authentication, session management, security protections, and load balancing. You cannot disable these cookies without losing access to core features.

**Examples:**
- Session identifiers that maintain your login state
- Security tokens that prevent cross-site request forgery
- Load balancer cookies that route requests to available servers

**Legal Basis:** Legitimate interest in providing the service you requested. These cookies do not require consent under the ePrivacy Directive.

### 3.2 Functional Cookies

These cookies enhance your experience by remembering preferences, interface settings, and workspace configurations. They are not strictly necessary but improve usability.

**Examples:**
- Theme preferences (light mode, dark mode)
- Language selection
- Sidebar collapse state
- Recently accessed documents

**Legal Basis:** Consent. You may disable these cookies through your browser settings, though doing so may reduce platform convenience.

### 3.3 Analytics Cookies

These cookies help us understand how users interact with PolicyPack. We use analytics data to identify performance bottlenecks, measure feature adoption, and prioritize product improvements.

**Examples:**
- Page view counters
- Session duration trackers
- Feature interaction logs
- Error reporting identifiers

**Legal Basis:** Consent. We deploy analytics cookies only after you accept them through our consent banner. We do not use analytics cookies for cross-site tracking or behavioral profiling.

### 3.4 Advertising and Marketing Cookies

PolicyPack does not currently deploy advertising cookies on the policypack.org domain or within the customer workspace. We do not participate in real-time bidding, retargeting networks, or cross-site behavioral advertising.

If we introduce marketing cookies in the future, we will update this policy and obtain your explicit consent before deployment.

## 4. First-Party and Third-Party Cookies

**First-party cookies** are set by PolicyPack directly. We control their purpose, retention, and data handling.

**Third-party cookies** are set by external services integrated into our platform. These may include:

- **Payment processors** that handle subscription billing and transaction security
- **Infrastructure providers** that deliver content, prevent abuse, and ensure platform availability
- **Analytics services** that aggregate usage data for performance monitoring

We select third-party services based on their data protection standards and contractual commitments. We do not permit third parties to use cookies for purposes unrelated to the services they provide to PolicyPack.

## 5. Analytics and Attribution

PolicyPack uses analytics cookies to measure platform performance and user engagement. We collect data such as:

- Pages visited and time spent on each page
- Features accessed and actions completed
- Device type, browser version, and screen resolution
- Referral source and entry point
- Error messages and failed operations

We do not collect precise geolocation data, keystroke logs, or personally identifiable information through analytics cookies unless you are logged into your account, in which case we associate usage data with your account identifier for support and product improvement purposes.

We retain analytics data for up to twenty-four months. Aggregated and anonymized analytics may be retained indefinitely for historical trend analysis.

## 6. Consent and Controls

### 6.1 Consent Banner

When you first visit policypack.org, we display a consent banner that blocks non-essential cookies until you make a choice. You may:

- Accept all cookies
- Reject all non-essential cookies
- Customize your preferences by category

Your consent choice is stored in a strictly necessary cookie and honored across all pages and sessions. You may withdraw or modify your consent at any time by accessing the cookie preferences link in the website footer.

### 6.2 Browser Controls

You may block or delete cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to:

- Block all cookies
- Block third-party cookies only
- Delete cookies after each session
- Receive notifications before cookies are set

Disabling strictly necessary cookies will prevent you from logging into your PolicyPack account or accessing authenticated features.

### 6.3 Do Not Track Signals

Some browsers transmit "Do Not Track" signals to websites. PolicyPack honors these signals by disabling analytics and functional cookies for users who enable this setting. Strictly necessary cookies remain active to maintain platform functionality.

### 6.4 Global Privacy Control

PolicyPack recognizes Global Privacy Control signals as a valid opt-out mechanism under the California Consumer Privacy Act and similar regulations. If your browser sends a GPC signal, we will not deploy analytics or marketing cookies and will not share your data for cross-context behavioral advertising purposes.

## 7. Cookie Retention Periods

Each cookie has a defined retention period:

- **Session cookies** expire when you close your browser
- **Authentication cookies** expire after fourteen days of inactivity
- **Preference cookies** expire after twelve months
- **Analytics cookies** expire after twenty-four months

You may delete cookies manually at any time through your browser settings. Deleting authentication cookies will log you out of your account.

## 8. International Data Transfers

Cookies may transmit data to servers located outside your jurisdiction. PolicyPack and our third-party service providers process cookie data in secure data centers located in the United States, European Union, and Singapore.

Where we transfer data from the European Economic Area or United Kingdom to jurisdictions without an adequacy decision, we rely on Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission and the UK Information Commissioner's Office.

## 9. Children's Privacy

PolicyPack does not knowingly collect data from individuals under the age of sixteen through cookies or any other mechanism. Our platform is designed for business users and SaaS operators. If we become aware that we have collected data from a child without parental consent, we will delete that data promptly.

## 10. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Cookie Policy to reflect changes in our practices, legal requirements, or platform features. We will post the updated policy on this page with a revised "Last Updated" date.

If we introduce new cookie categories or materially expand data collection practices, we will notify you through a prominent banner on the website and request renewed consent where required by law.

We recommend reviewing this policy periodically to stay informed about how we use cookies.

## 11. Contact and Data Protection Rights

If you have questions about our use of cookies, wish to withdraw consent, or want to exercise your data protection rights, contact us at support@policypack.org.

Under the General Data Protection Regulation and equivalent laws, you have the right to:

- Access the data we collect through cookies
- Request deletion of cookie data associated with your account
- Object to analytics or marketing cookies
- Withdraw consent for non-essential cookies
- Lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority

We will respond to requests within thirty days and provide clear instructions for exercising your rights.

For detailed information about how we collect, use, and protect your personal data beyond cookies, please review our Privacy Policy at https://policypack.org/privacy.
pages.refundPolicy:
**Operator:** Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack  
**Effective Date:** April 10, 2026  
**Contact:** support@policypack.org

PolicyPack provides digital legal automation services, including AI-generated compliance documents and premium subscription access. This Refund Policy explains when refunds are available, how to request them, and how we process refund claims. By purchasing any PolicyPack service or subscription, you agree to the terms set forth below.

## 1. Nature of Digital Products

PolicyPack delivers digital products and services that are made available to you immediately upon purchase or subscription activation. These include:

- **Generated Legal Documents:** Privacy policies, terms of service, cookie policies, refund policies, and related compliance disclosures produced through our platform and delivered in digital formats including markdown, HTML, and PDF.
- **Premium Subscriptions:** Ongoing access to advanced features, document updates, multi-site management, priority support, and enhanced export capabilities.
- **One-Time Exports:** Single-use document generation and export services purchased outside of a subscription plan.

All products are delivered electronically. No physical goods are shipped. Once a document is generated, exported, or a subscription period begins, the service has been performed and the digital product has been delivered.

## 2. General Refund Policy

Due to the immediate delivery and digital nature of our products, **refunds are generally not available** once:

- A legal document has been generated and made available for download or export.
- A premium subscription has been activated and access to premium features has been granted.
- A one-time export has been completed and the resulting file has been delivered.

This policy applies regardless of whether you have used, published, or implemented the generated documents. The service is considered fulfilled when the digital product is made accessible to you.

## 3. Subscription Billing and Cancellation

If you hold an active subscription:

- **Cancellation:** You may cancel your subscription at any time through your account settings or by contacting support@policypack.org. Cancellation will take effect at the end of your current billing period. You will retain access to premium features until that date.
- **No Pro-Rata Refunds:** Cancelling a subscription does not entitle you to a refund for the unused portion of the current billing cycle. You are responsible for the full subscription fee for the period in which you cancel.
- **Auto-Renewal:** Subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled before the renewal date. If you cancel after a renewal charge has been processed, that charge is non-refundable under this general policy.

## 4. Exceptions and Verified Defects

We will consider refund requests in the following limited circumstances:

- **Technical Failure:** If our platform fails to deliver a purchased document or grant subscription access due to a verified technical defect on our side, and we are unable to resolve the issue within a reasonable timeframe.
- **Duplicate Charges:** If you are charged multiple times for the same transaction due to a processing error, we will refund the duplicate charge.
- **Unauthorized Transactions:** If you report an unauthorized charge within fourteen days and provide sufficient evidence that the transaction was not initiated by you or an authorized user of your account.
- **Material Service Defect:** If a generated document contains a critical structural defect that renders it unusable for its stated purpose, and the defect is directly attributable to a platform malfunction rather than user input or customization choices.

Refund exceptions are evaluated on a case-by-case basis. We reserve the right to request documentation, screenshots, or other evidence to verify the claim. Refunds granted under this section will be issued to the original payment method.

## 5. Refund Request Process

To request a refund under the exceptions listed in Section 4, you must:

1. **Submit a Request:** Contact support@policypack.org within fourteen days of the transaction date. Include your account email, transaction ID or receipt, and a detailed explanation of the issue.
2. **Provide Evidence:** Attach relevant documentation such as error messages, screenshots, or payment receipts that support your claim.
3. **Allow Investigation Time:** Our support team will review your request and may ask follow-up questions or request additional information. We aim to respond to refund inquiries within five business days.

Refund requests submitted more than fourteen days after the transaction date will not be considered except in cases of unauthorized charges or billing errors that were not reasonably discoverable within that window.

## 6. Review and Resolution

Once we receive your refund request:

- **Evaluation:** We will investigate the claim, verify the reported issue, and determine whether it qualifies under the exceptions in Section 4.
- **Decision:** You will receive a written response via email explaining whether your refund request has been approved or denied, along with the reasoning.
- **Processing Time:** If approved, refunds will be processed within ten business days. The time it takes for the refund to appear in your account depends on your payment provider and may take an additional five to ten business days.
- **Partial Refunds:** In some cases, we may offer a partial refund, account credit, or alternative resolution such as re-generating a document or extending subscription access.

Our refund decisions are final. If you disagree with the outcome, you may escalate the matter through the dispute resolution process outlined in our Terms of Service.

## 7. Payment Provider Policies

PolicyPack processes payments through third-party payment providers. In addition to this Refund Policy, you may be subject to the refund and dispute resolution policies of:

- **Stripe:** Disputes and chargebacks are handled according to Stripe's policies. Initiating a chargeback without first contacting PolicyPack support may result in account suspension.
- **Paddle:** Paddle acts as the merchant of record for certain transactions and may apply its own refund policies in compliance with consumer protection laws in your jurisdiction.
- **PayPal and Braintree:** Transactions processed through PayPal or Braintree are subject to PayPal's buyer protection and dispute resolution procedures.

If you initiate a chargeback or payment dispute through your bank or payment provider without first attempting to resolve the issue with PolicyPack, we reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account and withhold access to any purchased products or services.

## 8. Consumer Rights and Jurisdiction-Specific Protections

This Refund Policy does not limit any statutory consumer rights you may have under the laws of your jurisdiction:

- **European Union and United Kingdom:** If you are a consumer located in the EU or UK, you may have a right to cancel digital content purchases within fourteen days under the Consumer Rights Directive and the Consumer Rights Act 2015. However, by expressly requesting immediate access to generated documents or premium features, you acknowledge that the service is performed in full and waive your right of withdrawal for that specific purchase.
- **Singapore:** The Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act applies to consumer transactions. This Refund Policy does not exclude liability for misleading conduct or unfair practices.
- **United States:** No federal law grants a general right of withdrawal for digital services. State-specific consumer protection laws may apply.

If any provision of this Refund Policy conflicts with mandatory consumer protection laws in your jurisdiction, those laws will prevail to the extent of the conflict.

## 9. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Refund Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our services, payment methods, or legal requirements. When we make material changes, we will update the Effective Date at the top of this page and notify active subscribers via email or account dashboard notice.

Your continued use of PolicyPack services after a policy update constitutes acceptance of the revised terms. If you do not agree with the updated Refund Policy, you may cancel your subscription or discontinue use of our services.

## 10. Contact and Support

If you have questions about this Refund Policy, need assistance with a refund request, or wish to report a billing issue, please contact us at:

**Email:** support@policypack.org  
**Website:** https://policypack.org

We are committed to resolving refund inquiries fairly and promptly. Our support team is available to assist you and will work with you to address any legitimate concerns regarding your purchase.
pages.aboutUs:
**Operator:** Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack  
**Jurisdiction:** Singapore  
**Website:** https://policypack.org  
**Contact:** support@policypack.org  
**Last Updated:** April 10, 2026

---

PolicyPack is built by Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd., a Singapore-based technology company founded to solve a specific problem: legal compliance should not be a barrier to building software.

We started PolicyPack because we saw talented founders spending days researching privacy regulations, copying templates from outdated blog posts, and worrying whether their Terms of Service would pass app store review. We believed there was a better way to handle the legal infrastructure that every digital product needs.

## Who We Are

Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. is a registered Singapore company operating the PolicyPack platform. We are a small, focused team that combines expertise in software engineering, regulatory compliance, and product design. Our work is guided by a simple principle: legal automation should be precise, transparent, and accessible to operators who are not lawyers.

We do not practice law. We do not provide legal advice. We build software that helps you generate compliance documents based on your specific business model, jurisdiction, and platform requirements.

## What PolicyPack Does

PolicyPack is an AI-powered legal automation platform designed for SaaS companies, website operators, payment providers, and app developers. The platform generates Privacy Policies, Terms of Service, Cookie Policies, Refund Policies, and related compliance disclosures tailored to your product and regulatory environment.

The system works by collecting structured information about your service—what data you collect, how you process payments, which platforms you operate on, where your users are located—and producing publication-ready legal documents that reflect current requirements under GDPR, UK GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, ePrivacy Directive, and platform-specific policies enforced by Apple App Store, Google Play, Stripe, Paddle, and PayPal.

PolicyPack is built for founders who need to move quickly without compromising on compliance quality. Documents are generated in clean markdown, ready to publish on your website or submit to app reviewers. Premium features include export formats, version tracking, and update notifications when regulations or platform policies change.

## Why Legal Automation Matters

Legal compliance is not optional. Privacy policies are required by law in the European Union, United Kingdom, California, and dozens of other jurisdictions. App stores reject submissions without functional legal pages. Payment processors suspend accounts that lack clear refund policies. Regulators issue fines for vague or incomplete disclosures.

The traditional options are expensive or risky. Hiring a law firm costs thousands of dollars and takes weeks. Copying a template from the internet often produces documents that do not match your actual data practices, creating liability rather than reducing it. Ignoring the problem entirely exposes you to enforcement actions, customer complaints, and platform bans.

PolicyPack exists to provide a third option: automated generation of compliance documents that are specific to your service, current with regulatory expectations, and produced at a cost structure that works for early-stage companies.

We do not replace lawyers for complex transactions, litigation, or high-risk regulatory matters. We replace the manual, error-prone process of drafting standard compliance documents from scratch.

## How We Think About AI and Compliance

PolicyPack uses secure automated data processors to generate legal documents. These systems analyze your service profile, match it against regulatory requirements and platform policies, and produce structured legal text that reflects best practices observed across thousands of SaaS companies.

We are transparent about the role of automation in our platform. AI systems assist in drafting, but the underlying logic is built on regulatory research, platform documentation, and enforcement patterns. Every document generated by PolicyPack is designed to meet the specific disclosure obligations that apply to your service.

We do not train AI models on customer data. We do not share your service details with third parties for marketing or analytics purposes. The information you provide to PolicyPack is used solely to generate your compliance documents.

When we rely on third-party infrastructure for processing, we select providers that meet enterprise security and privacy standards. We maintain a public sub-processor list and notify customers before adding new processors.

PolicyPack itself is subject to the same compliance obligations we help our customers meet. Our own Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, and Cookie Policy are published at https://policypack.org and reflect the standards we apply to customer-facing documents.

## Our Commitment to Quality

We take accuracy seriously. PolicyPack documents are built from regulatory text, platform guidelines, and enforcement precedents. We monitor changes to privacy laws, app store policies, and payment provider requirements. When a regulation is updated or a platform changes its review criteria, we update our generation logic and notify affected customers.

We do not guarantee that any document will be legally sufficient for your specific circumstances. We do not guarantee that app reviewers will approve your submission or that regulators will find your disclosures adequate. Legal compliance depends on your actual data practices, not just the text of your policy.

What we do guarantee is that PolicyPack documents reflect current regulatory expectations and platform requirements as of the date they are generated. We provide clear version tracking, update notifications, and support for customers who need to revise their policies as their service evolves.

## Who We Serve

PolicyPack is used by SaaS founders launching their first product, established companies expanding into new regions, payment providers onboarding merchants, and app developers preparing for store review.

Our customers operate globally. We serve companies based in the United States, United Kingdom, European Union, Singapore, and other jurisdictions. PolicyPack documents are designed to meet the compliance expectations of users in all major markets.

We work with businesses that process payments through Stripe, Paddle, PayPal, and Braintree. We support apps distributed through Apple App Store and Google Play. We help websites comply with GDPR cookie consent requirements and CCPA opt-out obligations.

If you operate a digital service that collects user data, processes payments, or distributes software through a platform with legal requirements, PolicyPack is built for you.

## Contact

For questions about PolicyPack, partnership inquiries, or support requests, contact us at support@policypack.org.

For privacy requests, including data access, correction, or deletion, use the same email address. We respond to all inquiries within 72 hours.

Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. is registered in Singapore. Our platform operates at https://policypack.org.
pages.legalDisclaimer:
**Operator:** Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack  
**Effective Date:** April 10, 2026  
**Jurisdiction:** Singapore  
**Contact:** support@policypack.org

---

## 1. Nature of Service

PolicyPack is an AI-powered legal automation platform operated by Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. The service generates draft legal documents, compliance disclosures, and policy templates for SaaS companies, website operators, and digital service providers. All content produced by PolicyPack, including Privacy Policies, Terms of Service, Cookie Policies, Refund Policies, and related materials, is provided for informational and drafting purposes only.

PolicyPack does not provide legal advice. The platform does not substitute for consultation with a qualified attorney licensed in your jurisdiction. Use of PolicyPack does not create an attorney-client relationship between you and Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd., its employees, contractors, or any affiliated legal professionals.

---

## 2. No Attorney-Client Relationship

No attorney-client relationship is formed by:

- Accessing or using the PolicyPack platform at policypack.org
- Generating, downloading, or publishing documents through PolicyPack
- Communicating with PolicyPack support at support@policypack.org
- Subscribing to PolicyPack services or purchasing premium exports
- Reading PolicyPack documentation, guides, or educational content

Any communication with PolicyPack support is administrative in nature and does not constitute legal consultation. PolicyPack support staff are not authorized to provide legal advice, interpret regulations, or recommend specific legal strategies for your business.

---

## 3. Professional Review Required

Documents generated by PolicyPack are drafts. They require review, customization, and approval by a qualified attorney before publication or reliance. PolicyPack cannot account for:

- Jurisdiction-specific legal requirements beyond the general frameworks incorporated into its drafting logic
- Industry-specific regulations applicable to your business model, such as financial services compliance, healthcare privacy rules, or telecommunications licensing
- Contractual obligations imposed by your vendors, partners, payment processors, or platform providers
- Recent regulatory changes, enforcement actions, or court decisions that postdate the platform's last update
- Factual circumstances unique to your operations, data flows, or customer relationships

Failure to obtain professional legal review may result in non-compliance with applicable laws, rejection by app store reviewers, disputes with payment providers, regulatory enforcement actions, or customer complaints.

---

## 4. Customer Responsibility

You are solely responsible for:

- Verifying the accuracy and completeness of all information you provide to PolicyPack during document generation
- Customizing generated documents to reflect your actual business practices, data processing activities, and service terms
- Ensuring that published policies match your operational reality, including third-party integrations, data retention practices, and customer rights procedures
- Monitoring changes in applicable laws and updating your policies accordingly
- Obtaining independent legal advice before publishing or relying on any PolicyPack output
- Complying with all legal obligations in jurisdictions where you operate, collect data, or serve customers

PolicyPack does not monitor your use of generated documents. We do not notify you of legal changes that may affect your published policies. You must establish your own compliance monitoring and policy update procedures.

---

## 5. No Guarantee of Regulatory Sufficiency

PolicyPack does not guarantee that documents generated through the platform will:

- Satisfy the requirements of any specific regulatory authority, including but not limited to data protection authorities in the European Union, United Kingdom, California, or Singapore
- Achieve approval from app store reviewers at Apple App Store or Google Play
- Meet the onboarding or compliance standards of payment processors such as Stripe, Paddle, PayPal, or Braintree
- Protect you from legal claims, regulatory investigations, or enforcement actions
- Constitute a complete or sufficient compliance program for your business

Regulatory expectations vary by jurisdiction, industry, and enforcement priorities. App store review guidelines and payment provider policies change frequently. PolicyPack drafting logic reflects general best practices and common legal frameworks as of the effective date of this disclaimer, but cannot predict how any specific reviewer, regulator, or adjudicator will interpret your published policies.

---

## 6. Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. disclaims all liability for:

- Legal claims, regulatory penalties, or financial losses arising from your use of PolicyPack-generated documents
- Rejection of your application by app stores, payment providers, or other platform operators due to policy deficiencies
- Data breaches, privacy violations, or consumer complaints related to discrepancies between your published policies and actual practices
- Errors, omissions, or inaccuracies in generated documents, whether caused by platform logic, user input, or third-party data sources
- Reliance on PolicyPack content without obtaining independent legal review

This limitation applies regardless of the legal theory asserted, including negligence, breach of contract, or strict liability. Some jurisdictions do not allow exclusion of liability for certain types of harm. In such jurisdictions, our liability is limited to the maximum extent permitted by law.

---

## 7. Third-Party Links and Resources

PolicyPack may reference or link to third-party legal resources, regulatory guidance, or compliance tools. These references are provided for informational purposes only. Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. does not endorse, verify, or assume responsibility for the accuracy, completeness, or reliability of third-party content. You access third-party resources at your own risk.

---

## 8. Changes to This Disclaimer

We may update this Legal Disclaimer to reflect changes in our services, legal obligations, or operational practices. Material changes will be posted at policypack.org with a revised effective date. Your continued use of PolicyPack after such changes constitutes acceptance of the updated disclaimer.

---

## 9. Contact Information

For questions about this Legal Disclaimer or PolicyPack services, contact:

**Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack**  
Email: support@policypack.org  
Website: https://policypack.org

For legal advice regarding your specific compliance obligations, consult a qualified attorney licensed in your jurisdiction.
pages.contactUs:
**Operator:** Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack  
**Contact Email:** support@policypack.org  
**Website:** https://policypack.org  
**Last Updated:** April 10, 2026

---

## 1. General Support

For questions about your PolicyPack account, document generation, publishing workflows, or technical assistance, contact our support team at support@policypack.org. We monitor this address during business hours and aim to respond to all inquiries within 48 hours on business days.

When writing to us, please include your account email address and a brief description of your question or issue. If your inquiry relates to a specific document or export, include the document type and any relevant error messages. This helps us route your request efficiently and provide accurate guidance.

Our support team can assist with:

- Account setup and configuration
- Document generation and customization
- Publishing and export workflows
- Integration questions
- Feature requests and feedback
- Technical troubleshooting

For urgent technical issues affecting live production environments, please mark your subject line with "URGENT" and include details about the impact. We prioritize requests that affect published compliance pages or active customer-facing services.

---

## 2. Privacy and Compliance Requests

If you have questions about how PolicyPack processes your personal data, wish to exercise your data protection rights, or need to submit a formal privacy request, contact us at support@policypack.org with "Privacy Request" in the subject line.

We handle the following privacy and compliance inquiries:

- **Data Subject Access Requests (DSAR):** Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you
- **Rectification Requests:** Correct inaccurate or incomplete personal data in your account
- **Erasure Requests:** Request deletion of your account and associated personal data
- **Restriction Requests:** Limit how we process your personal data in specific circumstances
- **Portability Requests:** Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
- **Objection Requests:** Object to processing based on legitimate interests or direct marketing
- **Consent Withdrawal:** Withdraw consent for processing activities that require your explicit agreement

We respond to verified privacy requests within 30 days under GDPR and UK GDPR, and within 45 days under CCPA and CPRA. If we require additional time due to request complexity, we will notify you of the extension and the reason for the delay.

For enterprise customers with executed Data Processing Agreements, please direct compliance inquiries to your designated account contact or use the DPA contact details provided in your agreement.

If you believe we have not adequately addressed your privacy concern, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority. For users in the European Union, contact details for supervisory authorities are available at https://edpb.europa.eu/about-edpb/about-edpb/members_en. For users in the United Kingdom, contact the Information Commissioner's Office at https://ico.org.uk.

---

## 3. Billing and Commercial Inquiries

For questions about subscriptions, invoices, payment methods, plan changes, or commercial partnerships, contact support@policypack.org with "Billing" or "Commercial" in the subject line.

Our billing team can assist with:

- Subscription plan selection and upgrades
- Invoice requests and payment receipts
- Payment method updates and billing address changes
- Refund requests and cancellation processing
- Enterprise licensing and volume pricing
- Reseller and partnership inquiries

If you are requesting a refund, please include your account email, the date of purchase, and a brief explanation of your request. We process refund requests in accordance with our published Refund Policy, which is available at https://policypack.org/refund-policy. Approved refunds are typically processed within 5 to 10 business days and will appear in your original payment method.

For enterprise customers interested in custom agreements, dedicated support, or volume licensing, include your company name, approximate team size, and a summary of your requirements. We will route your inquiry to our commercial team for a tailored proposal.

---

## 4. Response Times and Availability

We aim to respond to all inquiries within 48 hours on business days, Monday through Friday, excluding public holidays observed in Singapore. Urgent technical issues affecting live production environments receive priority attention and are typically acknowledged within 24 hours.

Complex requests, including privacy requests requiring data retrieval or legal review, may require additional processing time. We will acknowledge your request promptly and provide an estimated timeline for resolution.

If you do not receive a response within the expected timeframe, please check your spam or junk folder and ensure that emails from policypack.org are not being filtered. If you still have not received a reply, send a follow-up message referencing your original inquiry.

For the fastest resolution, please send all inquiries to support@policypack.org rather than attempting to contact individual team members directly. This ensures your request is logged, tracked, and routed to the appropriate specialist.

---

**Superlinear Technology Pte. Ltd. trading as PolicyPack**  
Email: support@policypack.org  
Website: https://policypack.org
